global.title: document model vs document editor vs app serves different purposes
global.content:
# document model vs document editor vs app serves different purposes

Three distinct document types serve different needs:

| Type | Purpose | Document Type |
|------|---------|---------------|
| **Document Model** | Defines structure — schema and operations for a document type | `powerhouse/document-model` |
| **Document Editor** | UI for editing a single document instance | `powerhouse/document-editor` |
| **App (Drive Editor)** | UI for managing collections of documents in a drive | `powerhouse/app` |

The critical distinction: when a user says "create an app" or "manage multiple documents," create a `powerhouse/app`, NOT a `powerhouse/document-model`.

Since [[apps use useSelectedDrive to access drive state and filter nodes]], apps work at the drive level. Since [[editors use the modern React hooks pattern from reactor-browser]], editors work at the individual document level.

---

Topics:
- [[architecture decisions]]
global.description: Document models define structure; editors provide UI for single documents; apps manage collections in a drive
